q: Titanium is an integral part of modern construction because it is highly corrosion-resistant, strong, and lightweight.
Titanium is used for roofs, flooring, and ceiling decoration in buildings.
Iron is usually mixed with small amounts of other elements to make harder alloy steels with greater tensile strength.
Manganese steel is extremely strong and is used for railway tracks.
Vanadium is used to form alloys of steel with iron.
$Vanadium(V)$ oxide ($V_2O_5$) is used as a catalyst in the ceramics industry.
$Vanadium(V)$ oxide is used as a mordant in the printing and dyeing of fabrics.
Chromium is used to manufacture stainless steel due to its toughness and resistance to heat.
Various chromium salts are the main raw materials in the chemical industry.
Chromium is involved in the production of car brake pads in the automobile industry.
Manganese is used as a catalyst to reduce the octane rating in gasoline.
Manganese is used in dry cell batteries and paint.
Cobalt ion has a brilliant blue colour used as paint, ink, and pigments.
Rechargeable batteries are made up of chromium.
Nickel is used for anti-corrosion coating and nickel plating.
Nickel is primarily used in the production of stainless steel.
Copper is a good conductor of electricity and is used for electrical wire and circuit boards.
Iron is used in modern medicine to treat or prevent anemia.
Copper has been used to treat Wilson’s disease and Menkes disease.
Surgical instruments are made of transition metals such as stainless steel and cobalt-chrome alloys.
Titanium surgical instruments are used because they are lightweight and corrosion-resistant.
Titanium has been used mostly for fabricating artificial hearts.
Titanium is used in artificial hip bone replacements and eyeglass frames.
Gemstones are found in different colours due to the presence of transition metal ions.
The red colour of ruby is due to a trace amount of chromium.
The blue colour of sapphire is due to a trace amount of manganese.
Transition metals like iron, chromium, copper, manganese, and zinc are essential trace elements for human beings.
Copper is used as a fungicide in the form of Bordeaux mixture.
Nichrome wire is an alloy made by mixing chromium and nickel.
